deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.name: Waycross Industrial
thesis: Class A 217,000 SF industrial building acquired at $52/SF, roughly 30% below replacement cost.
Former tenant-owned and recently vacated, creating an attractive entry basis.
Vertically integrated sponsor with deep industrial experience, sourced through a known broker.
NOI supports combined debt service from day one. Light value-add plan, sale exit inside three years.
capitalProtection: 25% equity cushion above our position
Takeover and forced-sale rights if current pay portion is missed
Priority position in the capital stack — first dollars out

outcome.narrative: Performed to plan. The broker who sourced the deal also invested as an LP and remarked that our preferred-equity return outperformed his common equity — a clean illustration of the risk-adjusted advantages of sitting senior in the stack.

deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.name: College Park Multifamily Takeover
thesis: Prior owner forced to sell due to underperformance. Senior lender selected this sponsor to take the asset over and acquire below the lender's basis.
Sponsor is Atlanta-local with 20+ full-cycle exits and previously executed an identical strategy on an adjacent property.
Identical adjacent property is 100% leased at higher rents than this proforma — direct local proof of the thesis.
Large Atlanta MSA, near Hartsfield-Jackson and major employers, heavy workforce population, virtually zero deliveries in the immediate area.
capitalProtection: 30% equity cushion above our position at entry
Takeover and forced-sale rights
Sponsor with 20+ full-cycle exits
deals.watchListNote: Currently flagged on the watch list — original business plan was disrupted by seller fraud (fabricated financial statements, renovation work misrepresented). Aspen activated takeover rights, internal REO team stepped in as general contractor, reduced renovation costs ~35% (~$800K savings), accelerated timelines, and negotiated GP warrants. Senior lender granted a 12-month maturity extension in Q1 2026. Aspen targets 75% occupancy by end of February with full stabilization by mid-April. Principal remains fully recoverable per revised analysis; conservative ~$300K NAV write-down recorded. Position is less than 5% of fund assets.

deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.name: Bentonville Multifamily Development
thesis: Experienced Northwest Arkansas developer with $9M+ of common equity in the deal — meaningful skin in the game.
Bentonville is one of the fastest-growing cities in America (Walmart HQ), with construction starts at multi-year lows and continued in-migration.
Exit valuation modeled at $233K/door — recent sale comps support this as conservative.
Fund participates in upside via 0.5% of the sale price at exit.
capitalProtection: 38% equity cushion above our position at stabilization
Takeover and forced-sale rights
2-year construction window plus 1-year lease-up — proforma based on market rents, not stretched assumptions

deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.name: Lee's Summit Loan Assumption
thesis: Sponsor assuming an existing agency loan at 2.94% — a rate that is effectively unrepeatable today.
Senior LTV of only ~45%. Layering preferred equity on top to lift investor IRR while keeping combined leverage conservative.
Institutional-level sponsorship with deep industrial experience. Conservative business plan, shorter horizon (expected payoff inside two years).
capitalProtection: 40% equity cushion above our position
Forced-sale rights if the 8% current pay is not made
Senior debt at a below-market rate makes the asset durable through any rate environment

deals.name: Derby Neighborhood Retail
thesis: Two strip centers serving the majority of southwest Wichita, anchored by foot traffic from nearby Derby High School.
Derby's median household income is ~$80K, about 20% above the rest of Wichita.
Operational thesis (not construction): tenants on month-to-month leases paying ~10% below market with no expense recovery.
Operator plan: flip everyone to NNN leases at market rents.
capitalProtection: 33% equity cushion above our position at stabilization
Forced-foreclosure rights as second lien
Submarket vacancy of 2% with no new supply pipeline

outcome.narrative: Operator executed quickly. By May 2025 (eight months in), all tenants were on NNN leases at market and the property hit a 1.51x DSCR. That positioned the property for a refinance that fully repaid our position at month 15, almost two years ahead of the original 3-year term.

deals.name: Pryor / Ark City Multifamily Portfolio
thesis: Tulsa-based sponsor with extensive workforce-housing track record. 2010s-vintage portfolio in tight-supply submarkets.
Acquired at $70K/door with ~8% yield-on-cost — a basis play with proven rents in the area.

Pryor is home to one of the largest industrial parks in the country (and growing). Arkansas City sits next to one of the largest high-quality beef producers in the U.S., which is planning to double its facility.
Property cash-flows our preferred position from day one.
capitalProtection: 28% equity cushion above our position at stabilization
Takeover and forced-sale rights
Exit valuation modeled at $90K/door — recent comps support this as conservative
deals.updateNote: 10 months in: NOI is 15% ahead of underwriting, 97% occupied, light value-add capex completed on time and on budget. The Sponsor + Manager (Gorrell Hughes / ParaWest) on this deal is the same team operating Deal #9.

deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.name: Glendale Medical Office
thesis: Acquired at $6.8M — roughly 50% below replacement cost and 64% below the 2019 purchase price of $19M.
Prime location inside Denver's medical hub, with 2,900 hospital beds within a 5-mile radius.
Below-market in-place lease rates ($22 vs. $24 market) and a strong basis ($80 PSF below market) provide multiple paths to value creation.
Strategic capex to drive efficiency. Property was 50% occupied at entry — clear value-add lever.
capitalProtection: 25% equity cushion above our position at stabilization
Takeover and forced-sale rights
Acquisition basis materially below replacement cost

deals.name: MHC Fund 1
thesis: 450+ unit Class C manufactured-housing portfolio across Toledo, OH and Evansville, IN.
Cash-flowed day one at 1.18x DSCR. Year-end combined DSCR projected at 1.42x.
Proven operator with 28 total parks and full-cycle exits. $15M of verified sponsor liquidity, $115M combined sponsor net worth.
One-year mezzanine bridge designed to convert into a Delaware Statutory Trust (DST) at maturity, giving DST investors a passive 1031-exchange opportunity.
capitalProtection: 29% equity cushion above our position at stabilization
Full recourse with personal guarantees from each principal — a structural feature beyond the equity cushion
Below-market average site rents create operational lift

outcome.narrative: Performed in line with underwriting and exited cleanly at maturity. No principal impairment, full accrued interest paid through. First mezzanine-debt full cycle for the fund — the prior two payoffs were preferred equity — validating the structural protections and execution discipline applied to debt positions as well as equity.

deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.name: Excelsior Sunset Portfolio
thesis: Same Sponsor + Property Manager (Gorrell Hughes / ParaWest) as Deal #6. 10 months into the prior deal: NOI is 15% ahead of underwriting, properties 97% occupied, light value-add capex completed on time and on budget.
281 units across two stabilized garden-style properties (Sunset Woodland, 177 units in Springfield; Excelsior Gardens, 104 units in Ozark). Both already over 90% occupied.
Acquired at $97K/unit — roughly 75% of replacement cost. Going-in 6.05% in-place cap rate, projected Year 1 NOI yield of 8.19%.

Workforce-housing tailwinds: Springfield MSA is one of the fastest-growing in Missouri, anchored by CoxHealth, Mercy Hospital, O'Reilly Auto HQ, and Missouri State University. Cost of living 15% below national average. Limited new multifamily supply.

Operational upside, not construction risk. Modest capex (~$6K/unit). Real lever is a full RUBS rollout (utility reimbursements projected up ~250%) plus mark-to-market rents and tax/insurance efficiencies. Sponsor models 35% NOI uplift over the hold.
capitalProtection: Breakeven basis of $98.7K/unit — well below recent comps, providing meaningful cushion in a soft exit
$280K interest reserve funded at closing covers 7-8 months of current pay
18-month minimum return floor on early redemption
Late payments accrue a +5% penalty rate (16% base → 21%), retroactive to the due date
Protective Capital right: if the Sponsor fails to fund a shortfall, Aspen can step in with a Protective Capital Contribution earning 21% annual return
Refinance can't dodge fees: 4% of net equity proceeds and 2% on invested capital follow the deal even through a refinance
Underwriting models a 19.01% IRR even in a full LP-equity wipeout scenario

deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.name: Lakeview Climate-Controlled Storage
thesis: Supply-constrained market: below national-average self-storage saturation, less than 1% annual supply growth, no new construction within five miles. Physical barriers (Lake Pontchartrain, 17th Street Canal) cap future development.

Proven sponsor: Verdad has developed 880K+ SF across 12 self-storage facilities. Transitioning operations to CubeSmart (top-3 national REIT) for institutional management, insurance revenue-sharing, and dynamic pricing.
Clear lease-up opportunity. Both phases delivered (2018 and 2023) — no construction risk. Currently 84% occupied with in-place rents at $13.76/SF versus ~$20/SF market.
Prime Metairie positioning between Orleans and Jefferson Parishes. 30K+ daily traffic on Veterans Boulevard, 312K residents within five miles, $123K+ average HHI within one-mile radius.
capitalProtection: Takeover and forced-sale rights at the structural level
Below-market in-place rents create immediate operational lift
Supply-constrained market limits competitive risk

deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.name: Victoria Multifamily Portfolio
thesis: 440-unit portfolio at 97% occupancy with a 1.21x going-in DSCR. $6M in renovations already complete, no additional capex required.
Fixed-rate Freddie Mac debt at 3.5% through November 2030 provides rate certainty and amortizing principal paydown — a senior-debt cost the sponsor could not source at today's rates.
Vertically integrated sponsor: TriArc owns and self-manages 2,500+ units (primarily Houston) and has put $2M of common equity into the deal.
Clear refinance path: Freddie Mac quoted $1.6M in supplemental loan proceeds available within 24-36 months — viable preferred-equity exit without selling into the current market.
capitalProtection: Takeover and forced-sale rights
Existing renovations already complete — no construction-risk exposure
Below-market senior debt locked through 2030

deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.name: Auden Upstate
thesis: Structural supply/demand imbalance: USC Upstate has 900 on-campus beds for 4,467+ undergraduates, no new student housing in the pipeline. Freshman enrollment up 16% YoY.
Auden's all-in cost ($1,070/month) undercuts the on-campus dorm-and-meal bundle by $240-485/month.
2020-vintage property with modern finishes — zero capex required. 1.26x going-in DSCR, 97% occupied, 85% preleased for fall 2025.

Operational upside: current foreign-based developer lacks U.S. student-housing experience and is charging below-market rents on a premium product. Transition to PeakMade (top-tier national operator, 290+ properties / 140K+ beds) creates a clear path for rent optimization.
Operator advantage: PeakMade already manages Valley Falls Apartments — the only other purpose-built student housing at USC Upstate — providing local staff and market intelligence.
capitalProtection: Takeover and forced-sale rights
Acquisition basis at $159K/unit / $56K/bed — well below replacement cost
Pre-leased fall 2025 occupancy backstops Year 1 cash flow

deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.notes: Additional deal terrms: 1.6x minimum equity multiple; 20% participation in GP promote
deals.name: Sky Park El Paso Industrial
thesis: Last major airport-adjacent site: the final significant developable parcel adjacent to El Paso International Airport, with direct I-10 access and Union Pacific intermodal connectivity.
Fully entitled with permits secured and site infrastructure (drainage, water, sewer, fiber) already in place.
Institutional specifications: 636,392 SF across two buildings (444K SF cross-dock + 192K SF rear-load). 36' clear heights, ESFR sprinklers, 71+ dock doors. CBRE Borderplex team leading leasing.
Favorable market timing: submarket supply pipeline contracted from 7.4M SF peak in 2022 to 3.7M SF currently. Recent absorption is positive (1.12M SF in Q2 2025 alone).
Cross-border nearshoring tailwind: El Paso is the primary U.S. logistics gateway for Ciudad Juarez maquiladora operations (4.9M SF currently under construction in Juarez).
Ground-lease tax efficiency: 49-year ground-lease structure eliminates property taxes on the land — saving ~$0.40-0.60 PSF/yr — with ground rent passed through to tenants as NNN.
capitalProtection: 20% equity cushion above our position
Takeover and forced-sale rights
1.6x minimum equity multiple guarantees a floor return regardless of deal outcome
20% participation in GP promote provides upside beyond the preferred return

deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.name: Compass at Windmill Lakes
thesis: Loan-basis acquisition: prior owner bought for $22M in 2022. Sponsor is acquiring at $16.5M (loan basis) — $110K/unit, 35% below estimated $190K/unit replacement cost.

1.12x going-in DSCR with combined LTC of 80%. Full breakeven return at a sale price below the acquisition basis. Modeled refinance in Year 5 produces excess proceeds after full preferred-equity repayment.

Recovering market with minimal new supply. Southeast Houston vacancy declining (13.1%, down from 13.8% Q4 2024 peak). Only 780 units under construction submarket-wide — the lowest pipeline since 2019, less than 1% of inventory.
New supply is concentrated near NASA and Baybrook Mall, away from the Southbelt corridor where the property sits.
Strategic location: minutes from Hobby Airport, sub-30-minute commutes to Texas Medical Center, Downtown Houston, Port of Houston, NASA/Johnson Space Center.
HM Equity Management: 25+ years of Houston-specific experience, $650M+ in acquisitions, 30+ full-cycle multifamily exits.
capitalProtection: Takeover and forced-sale rights
Acquisition at loan basis — 35% below replacement cost
Full breakeven return at a sale price below acquisition basis

deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.name: Cielo at West Midtown
thesis: Distressed seller, attractive basis: prior owner invested $14M (purchase + capex) and is exiting at $12.5M due to loan-maturity pressure. Going-in cap of 7.72% at $112K/unit — well below recent comps and replacement cost.
Renovations complete, limited capex risk. Comprehensive 2024 renovation invested $2.5M (~$22K/unit) into plumbing, electrical, HVAC, windows, and full interior upgrades.
93% occupied. Stabilized debt yield of 9.1%. Breakeven per door of $104K.

Proven rent achievement: 42% of units already within $50 of proforma rents. Leases signed since May 2025 average just $36 below proforma — renovated units are leasing at or near underwritten levels without aggressive growth assumptions.

Emerging submarket: West Midtown has transformed from industrial to a mixed-use district with restaurants, creative office, and experiential retail along Howell Mill Road. Adjacent to Westside Park at Bellwood Quarry — Atlanta's largest green space.
capitalProtection: Takeover and forced-sale rights
Distressed seller exit creates basis $1.5M below prior owner cost
Renovation capex already deployed and visible in unit-level rent results

deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.name: City Park Apartments
thesis: Distressed acquisition at a significant discount: sponsor (Two Waters Capital, 'TWC') acquired the 208-unit asset for $20.9M ($100,481/unit) through a lender-negotiated foreclosure — approximately a 40% discount to the prior owner's basis. Senior lender (Harbour) absorbed a ~$2M principal loss; all prior equity was wiped out.

Significant capex already completed. Prior ownership invested ~$5M (~$25K/unit) since 2021, substantially completing the heavy lift before the asset traded. TWC has budgeted only $500K for remaining make-readies.

Same Sponsor and Manager as Deal #15 (Cielo at West Midtown). Property management again sits with REMG Corp (TWC's in-house PM), giving the fund consistency of execution and proven local Atlanta expertise across both positions.
Going-in DSCR of 1.30x provides meaningful Year 1 cushion.

Operational right-sizing over heavy redevelopment: T-12 operating expense ratio ~66% of gross potential rent under prior management. Under REMG, expenses projected to normalize to ~53% of GPR — driven by vendor renegotiation, staffing optimization, and tighter expense controls, without aggressive rent-growth assumptions.
Half-mile west of I-285 and 1.5 miles south of I-20 — direct connectivity to Downtown Atlanta, Hartsfield-Jackson, and the Fulton Industrial Boulevard employment corridor.
capitalProtection: Takeover and forced-sale rights
Co-terminus with senior Freddie Mac maturity — no early-refinance pressure
Acquisition at a 40% discount to prior owner's basis
Heavy capex already completed before our entry

deals.security: Forced Sale and Take Over Rights
deals.name: Fayetteville Portfolio
thesis: Workforce-housing portfolio with anchor demand driver. 508-unit, 3-property Class B portfolio anchored by Fort Bragg — the largest U.S. Army installation by personnel, with ~53,000 active-duty soldiers and 100,000+ total military and civilian employees.
Military tenants receive Basic Allowance for Housing (BAH), creating a credit-worthy, cycle-resistant demand pool largely insensitive to broader economic cycles.
Significant discount to replacement cost: sponsor Valor Residential acquired from a distressed seller for $50.5M ($99,409/unit), well below the estimated $180K-200K/unit replacement cost.
Seller had already deployed $4.9M+ in capital improvements, partially de-risking the renovation component.
Vertically integrated sponsor: Valor Residential, ~$2B AUM, with a proven HUD refinance track record on similar Southeastern multifamily assets.
HUD refinance exit path provides clear visibility, with Aspen-modeled excess proceeds beginning in Year 3.

Conservative underwriting on a stabilized base: Aspen assumes 8-9% stabilized vacancy (versus 6-10% range typical for Class B in the submarket) and 90% portfolio occupancy — meaningfully more conservative than the 95%+ assumptions common across multifamily.

Operational value-add: bad-debt reduction (from ~10% of GPR to a stabilized 3-4%), vacancy reduction (from ~12% to sub-8%), and selective interior renovations to capture the ~$240K+ T-12 loss-to-lease.
capitalProtection: Takeover and forced-sale rights
Acquisition at significant discount to replacement cost
Sponsor with proven HUD refinance track record
Aspen's underwriting assumes vacancy and occupancy assumptions more conservative than market norms
